name: Data Processing Addendum 2024.pdf
text:
[Page 1]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
CTR-013  Data Processing Addendum 2024
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 1
DOCUMENT CONTROL
Data Processing Addendum 2024
Personal-data terms attached to the Northwind cloud-services engagement.
Document ID
CTR-013
Owner
Privacy Counsel
Version
1.0
Effective Date
2024-01-15
Controller
Meridian Holdings Ltd.
Processor
Northwind Cloud Systems LLC
Synthetic corporate document for demo, evaluation, and retrieval workflows only. All parties, people, systems, repositories, and events are
fictional.
1. Roles and Processing
●
Meridian acts as Controller for Customer Personal Data processed through the managed retrieval and analytics services.
●
Northwind acts as Processor except where applicable law requires Northwind to act independently.
●
Processor personnel with access to Customer Personal Data must be subject to confidentiality obligations and
background screening appropriate to role sensitivity.
2. Security Measures
Control Area
Requirement
Access Management
Least-privilege access, MFA for privileged users, quarterly recertification of production
administrators.
Encryption
Encryption in transit and at rest for production data stores and backups.
Monitoring
Security events and privileged actions retained for at least 12 months.
Deletion / Return
Return or delete Customer Personal Data within 30 days after termination unless retention is
required by law.
3. International Transfers and Data Location
Clause 5.2 — Data Location. Processor may process Customer Personal Data in any jurisdiction where Processor, its
Affiliates, or its approved subprocessors maintain facilities, support personnel, or technical infrastructure reasonably
required to deliver the Services.
Clause 5.3 — Applicable-Law Compliance. Processor shall implement reasonable measures intended to support lawful
international transfers and shall cooperate with Customer where supplemental documentation is materially necessary.
Clause 5.4 — Transfer Mechanism. As of the Effective Date, the parties acknowledge that specific transfer appendices,
including any standard contractual clauses, may be added later if requested by Customer and agreed in writing.
Gap intentionally preserved: the DPA does not require EU-only residency and does not attach an SCC
appendix. This supports the compliance finding and checklist action.

[Page 2]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
CTR-013  Data Processing Addendum 2024
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 2
4. Subprocessors and Assistance
Clause 6.1 — Subprocessors. Processor will maintain a current subprocessor list and will make updates available through
its supplier portal. Processor will consider commercially reasonable objections received within fifteen (15) days of
publication of a material change.
Clause 7.2 — Audit Assistance. Processor will provide reasonable information and assistance needed to demonstrate
compliance, including current certifications, summary reports, and targeted responses for material diligence matters.
Appendix Status
Appendix
Status
Comment
Appendix A — Processing Details
Attached
Service scope, categories of data, and subject groups included.
Appendix B — Security Measures
Attached
High-level control summary only.
Appendix C — Standard
Contractual Clauses
Reserved / not
attached
No executed SCC package included in the current file.
